mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent above-target inflation (CPIAUCSL > 250) signals central bank hawkishness, prompting rate hikes that depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both GDPC1 and INDPRO show growth.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary cycle (FEDFUNDS > 2%) combined with declining consumer spending (RSAFS) signals recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% and RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates a tightening labor market, increasing corporate wages and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST < 1300) signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1300.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE < 3.5%) combined with high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 250) indicates stagflation risk, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 3.5% and CPIAUCSL > 250.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production (INDPRO < 100) during low-rate environments (FEDFUNDS < 2%) signals supply shocks, creating inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO < 100 and FEDFUNDS < 2%.
